{{Quote|I'm Jinkins, Nym's chief engineer. Nym asks for weapons, and I build them.|Jinkins, to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:FJ">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Find Jinkins|Lok}}</ref>}}
'''Jinkins''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] who served as an [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] and general [[Aide/Legends|aide]] to the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] captain [[Nym/Legends|Nym]], in the years surrounding the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]].

==Biography==
===Fighting the Trade federation===
{{Quote|I joined up with the Lok Revenants a few months before the Battle of Naboo, which makes me one of this crew's oldest members, along with Kole of course.|Jinkins, to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:FJ" />}}
Jinkins was originally an engineer with the [[Nubia Star Drives, Incorporated/Legends|Nubian Design Collective]], and the lead engineer on the [[Scurrg H-6 Bomber/Legends|Scurrg H-6 bomber]], a prototype model designed for the [[Naboo Royal Space Fighter Corps/Legends|Naboo Space Fighter Corps]]. After the [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]] rejected the design, the [[Nubian]]s scrapped the project, though Jinkins was not about to let his work go to waste. He arranged a deal with the [[Feeorin/Legends|Feeorin]] pirate [[Nym/Legends|Nym]], who stole the prototype and took Jinkins along with it.

[[File:Jinkins.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Jinkins]]

Joining the [[Lok Revenants/Legends|Lok Revenants]], Jinkins served as sensor and scanner operator aboard the newly christened [[Havoc (starfighter)|''Havoc'']]. Though Jinkins worked aboard the ''Havoc'', he often piloted his own vessel, the ''[[Freefall]]''. After an unfortunate run-in with [[Vana Sage]], Jinkins and his wingmate [[Kole]] were jettisoned from the space station, and Nym was handed over to the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]]. Jinkins and Kole later infiltrated Nym's cell while masquerading as masters of torture.<ref name="Single Cell">{{Tales|7|Single Cell}}</ref> Jinkins also engaged in patrols around Nym's hideout on [[Lok/Legends|Lok]], though he was almost killed during one such scout mission that coincided with a full-scale invasion by the Trade Federation. The Revenants [[Battle of Lok (32 BBY)|fled]] Lok<ref name="Star Wars: Starfighter" /> but continued to harry the Federation for the next decade.

When the Revenants were on the run, they accepted odd jobs to survive, such as a movie on [[Rodia/Legends|Rodia]] or to hunt down the infamous pirate [[Sol Sixxa]]. During the hunt for Sixxa, Jinkins was the engineer on the ''[[Sunrunner]]''. Their hunt ended with the apparent death of Sixxa, although in reality he and Nym joined forces.<ref name="Star Wars: Starfighter: Crossbones">''[[Star Wars: Starfighter: Crossbones]]''</ref>

Jinkins remained in Nym's service, helping liberate [[Mere Resistance]] fighters from the infamous [[Spacestation 1138]], and later aided in the [[Battle of Nod Kartha|battle]] of [[Nod Kartha]] and the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|destruction]] of [[Cavik Toth]]'s Hex fleet.<ref name="SW:JF">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter]]''</ref>

===Galactic Civil War===
{{Quote|The miners hit some sort of gas pocket. The entire work crew had their brains boiled. They survived, but they're crazy. And the droid thinks that some of the Imperial officers were infected too. Any gas that drives Imperials out of their minds is a good thing to have around, so we want some samples of it.|Jinkins, to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:FJ" />}}
After the defeat of the Trade Federation, Jinkins and Kole remained loyal to Nym and stayed with him almost continually over the years,<ref name="Insider">{{InsiderCite|65|Who's Who in Star Wars Galaxies|Who's Who in ''Star Wars'' Galaxies}}</ref> with Jinkins serving as Nym's chief [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]].<ref name="SWG:FJ" /> Following the Clone Wars, Nym assumed the leadership of the planet Lok and ruled the planet for decades. At the height of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Lok had fallen under the control of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] but Nym remained the effective ruler of the planet. At that time, Jinkins was known as one of Lok's best engineers.<ref name="Insider" />

[[File:Lok_Revenants_leaders.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Jinkins with the other leaders of the Lok Revenants.]]
In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> an accident took place in a secret [[Imperial Mine (Lok)|Imperial mining facility]] on Lok. The miners hit a gas pocket and released toxic vapours in the mine. The gas did not physically injured the miners but made them crazy and very aggressive. The Lok Revenants discovered the situation after retrieving from the [[Sulfur Lake Pirates]] the [[memory module]] of an Imperial [[Droid/Legends|droid]] that escaped the mine. Jinkins then decided to obtain a sample of the gas to study it and use it as a weapon if possible.<ref name="SWG:FJ" />

Jinkins thus tasked a group of spacers recently recruited among the Lok Revenants by Vana Sage with retrieving a sample of toxic gas. The spacers therefore headed to the mine where they had to fight numerous mad and violent miners.<ref name="SWG:RtMF">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Retrieve the Mine Filter|Lok}}</ref> Using information obtained from the corrupted Imperial [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Moore]],<ref name="SWG:SwSM">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Speak with Sergeant Moore|Lok}}</ref> they managed to get rid of the Imperial [[Droideka/Legends|droidekas]] that defended the facility. Finally, the spacers found the air filtration system of the mine and retrieved a sealed container of the poisonous gas pumped by the filter. They eventually delivered it to Jinkins.<ref name="SWG:RtMF" />

In the meantime, the tensions between the Lok Revenants and the [[Sulfur Lake Pirates]], another criminal group active on Lok, turned to open war. After some setbacks,<ref name="SWG:NP">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Negotiate Peace|Lok}}</ref> the pirates set up an elaborate plan to directly attack Nym in his [[Nym's Base|palace]]. They hijacked a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] carrying an envoy of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooinan]] politicians to a meeting with Nym. As the shuttle landed in the courtyard of the palace, Nym and the other leaders of the Lok Revenants—Vana Sage, Kole and Jinkins—arrived to greet the Tatooinan envoy. A battle subsequently erupted in the palace when the Sulfur Lake Pirates emerged unexpectedly from the shuttle and attacked the Revenants. However, the superior fighting skills of Nym's henchmen allowed them to eliminate every Sulfur Lake Pirate. Nym, Jinkins, and the other leaders of the Lok Revenants survived the attack.<ref name="SWG:E">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Envoy|Lok}}</ref>

Around the same time, Jinkins designed an experimental [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]] that he named [[Jinkins J-1 rifle|J-1 rifle]].<ref name="SWG:JJR">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=item|Jinkins J-1 Rifle}}</ref> Tthe weapon was eventually awarded to one of the spacers who joined the Lok Revenants and provided exceptional service to Nym, Jinkins and the Revenants.<ref name="SWG:NTPCMC">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=collection|Nym's Theme Park Content Master Collection}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
A brilliant engineer, Jinkins hated it when good technology went to waste, going so far as to abscond with his design and join the Lok Revenants. After his defection, he went on to be a loyal companion to Nym.

==Behind the scenes==
If you fail your mission to save Jinkins during the assault on Nym's base in [[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']], Jinkins is killed during the invasion, though the game continues without him. Since the character reappears in [[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter|the sequel]], this part of the game must be non-[[Star Wars Legends|canonical]].

In the first game he speaks with an American accent, while in the sequel he speaks with an English accent, voiced by [[Ross King]].

In [[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']], Jinkins's name is misspelled "Jenkins." ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' mistakenly refers to Merick, the identity Jinkins assumes in "[[Single Cell]]," as an actual character and not an alias.

Jinkins was a [[Wikipedia:Non-player character|Non-Player Character]] in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:NTP" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> Jinkins was added to the game as part of the so-called "[[W:c:swg:Nym's Theme Park|Nym's Theme Park]],"<ref name="SWG:NTP" /> released with the "[[W:c:swg:Publish 3 Notes: 8 October 03|Publish 3]]" on [[October 8]], 2003.<ref name="SonyCite-Publish 3: The Warren">{{SonyCite|url=recent_updates.jsp?o=60890|text=Publish 3: The Warren}}</ref> His dialogues and quests were modified after Nym's Theme Park was revamped with the release of the "[[W:c:swg:Game Update 16|Game Update 16]]," on [[March 23]], [[2010]].<ref name="SonyCite-Game Update 16: ''Star Wars'' Galaxies™: Stronghold of the Lok Revenants">{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=68713&month=032010|text=Game Update 16: ''Star Wars'' Galaxies™: Stronghold of the Lok Revenants}}</ref>
